EDCS-08800024.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Epaphroditus, recording his lifespan and burial.

EDCS-08800036.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, possibly the beginning of a name and filiation or title.

EDCS-08800041.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Tacneidir, son of Securius, from Masaisulis, lived 45 years.
EDCS-08800041.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Tacneidir, son of Securius, recording his origin and lifespan.

EDCS-08800042.summary: This inscription is a dedication to the deified Valentinianus.

EDCS-08800043.translation: To the spirits of the departed.
EDCS-08800043.summary: This is a standard dedication to the 'Dis Manibus' (spirits of the dead).

EDCS-08800045.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed.lius Attianus from the Britons, to his most faithful young man, lived 24 years, 6 months, 10 days. Here he is laid; may the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-08800045.summary: This tombstone commemorateslius Attianus, a young man from Britannia, likely erected by his family or loved ones.

EDCS-08800048.translation: Julius Censor, dear to his own, 8 years old, is laid here; may the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-08800048.summary: This tombstone commemorates Julius Censor, who died at the age of 8.

EDCS-08800049.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Aelia Catura placed this tomb for her son, Julius Muntanus, aged 29.
EDCS-08800049.summary: This inscription records that Aelia Catura erected a tomb for her son, Julius Muntanus.

EDCS-08800050.translation: Ana, the mother, gave the gift at her own expense.
EDCS-08800050.summary: This inscription records a gift given by a mother named Ana at her own expense.

EDCS-08800057.translation: Lucius Gavius Proculus, 50 years old, is laid here.
EDCS-08800057.summary: This inscription marks the burial place of Lucius Gavius Proculus, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-08800058.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Julius Lucianus lived 5 years, 3 months, 16 days. Here he is laid; may the earth rest lightly on you.
EDCS-08800058.summary: This tombstone commemorates Lucius Julius Lucianus, who died at the age of 5.

EDCS-08800060.translation: The freedman of Fabia, Atimetus Augustalis, gave and dedicated this gift in honor of his patrons, from his own funds.
EDCS-08800060.summary: This inscription records that Atimetus Augustalis, a freedman, erected a gift from his own money in honor of his patrons.

EDCS-08800065.translation: onio, son of Marci, ilio, had the monument prepared and gave and dedicated the gift.
EDCS-08800065.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument and gift, likely by family members, foronio Marci filio ilio.

EDCS-08800070.translation: Caius Valerius Paternus, centurion of the Fifth Macedonian Legion, willingly and deservedly fulfilled his vow to the Genius.
EDCS-08800070.summary: This is a votive inscription by Caius Valerius Paternus, a centurion, fulfilling a vow to the Genius.

EDCS-08800071.translation: Sacred to Imperial Isis. Lucius Antonius Charito, freedman of Lucius Antonius Valentis, gave and dedicated this gift because of his office as a duovir.
EDCS-08800071.summary: This is a dedication to Imperial Isis by Lucius Antonius Charito, a freedman, in honor of his office as duovir.

EDCS-08800072.translation: Marcus Pompeius Saturninus and Valeria Fortunata, parents, set this up for Juno Augusta in honor of their daughter, Pompeia Valeriana.
EDCS-08800072.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Marcus Pompeius Saturninus and Valeria Fortunata for their daughter Pompeia Valeriana, in honor of Juno Augusta.

EDCS-08800073.translation: Sacred to Imperial Minerva. Marcus Terentius Primulus, freedman of Terentia Marciana, dedicated this gift because of his office as a duovir, and gave it from his own funds.
EDCS-08800073.summary: This is a dedication to Imperial Minerva by Marcus Terentius Primulus, a freedman, in honor of his office as duovir, funded by himself.

EDCS-08800076.summary: This inscription fragment appears to record the names of Roman emperors, possibly Nerva and Trajan, from the late 1st or early 2nd century AD.

EDCS-08800093.summary: This is a dedication to the Pontifex Maximus, likely referring to the emperor, with an epithet emphasizing his invincibility.

EDCS-08800114.translation: Aelius, freedman Seimo, gave and dedicated this gift because of his office as a duovir.
EDCS-08800114.summary: This is a dedication by Aelius Seimo, a freedman, in honor of his office as duovir.

EDCS-08800115.translation: Ulpia Cai filia Modesta set this up for her most dutiful mother, Caecilia Macrina, daughter of Lucius, wife of Libo, and priestess.
EDCS-08800115.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by Ulpia Modesta for her mother, Caecilia Macrina, who held positions as a priestess and was married to Libo.

EDCS-08800117.translation: Pacatus and Valeria Sa, mother of Marcus' daughter, honored and set this up from their own resources for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800117.summary: This is a funerary dedication set up by Pacatus and Valeria Sa for their son, likely during the 1st or 2nd century AD.

EDCS-08800119.translation: With Saturninus and Nianus as the two magistrates.
EDCS-08800119.summary: This inscription records the names of two local magistrates, Saturninus and Nianus, who were serving as duovirs.

EDCS-08800122.summary: This is a funerary inscription identifying the deceased by name as Quintus Caecilius.

EDCS-08800123.summary: This is a dedication or funerary inscription identifying the recipient or deceased by name as Quinto Caesio Victor Sabinianus.

EDCS-08800125.translation: Marcus Iulius, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe
EDCS-08800125.summary: This inscription identifies an individual, Marcus Iulius, by his name, patronymic, and tribal affiliation.

EDCS-08800126.summary: This is an ownership or dedication inscription stating that Tiberius Iulius Mercurialis created the object or monument.

EDCS-08800129.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Crispina, daughter of Marcus, lived for six years, three months, and twenty-seven days.
EDCS-08800129.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and age of Iulia Crispina, daughter of Marcus.

EDCS-08800131.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Saenia, dutiful, aged...
EDCS-08800131.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription for Saenia, indicating her piety and partial age.

EDCS-08800132.translation: from his own [property], the freedman Cocceius
EDCS-08800132.summary: This inscription indicates that an object or benefit was provided from the personal wealth of a freedman named Cocceius.

EDCS-08800165.summary: This is a fragmentary dedication or funerary inscription mentioning the name Valerius.

EDCS-08800221.translation: To Aquilo, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the Gemelliana cavalry wing, and duumvir of the colony of Babs
EDCS-08800221.summary: This is a dedication honoring Aquilo, detailing his military career as a veteran decurion and his civic role as a duumvir.

EDCS-08800222.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelius Arruntius, formerly a decurion and veteran, lived for 60 years. May the earth rest lightly upon you.
EDCS-08800222.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death and age of Aurelius Arruntius, a veteran decurion.

EDCS-08800223.translation: Lucius Nigidius Albanus, a veteran, is buried here; his heir Valerius Corsicus had this made according to the will.
EDCS-08800223.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of a veteran, Lucius Nigidius Albanus, and commissioned by his heir.

EDCS-08800225.translation: Agatta made this for her most devoted husband, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-08800225.summary: A funerary epitaph for a husband, made by his devoted wife, with his lifespan.

EDCS-08800226.translation: Sacred to the gods of the underworld. Iucuta lived for approximately 16 years; her husband placed this.
EDCS-08800226.summary: A funerary epitaph dedicated to the underworld gods, recording the age of Iucuta and placed by her husband.

EDCS-08800227.translation: Iulia Quieta placed this, sacred to the gods of the underworld. She lived 16 years and 56 days, a devoted wife in her own property; she is buried here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08800227.summary: A funerary epitaph for Iulia Quieta, who lived 16 years and 56 days, and is buried on her own property, with a final wish for her rest.

EDCS-08800230.translation: Whatever traveler passing by reads this and seeks your fate, know that I, Valeria Daca, was here, a most devoted wife at 18 years of age. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08800230.summary: A funerary epitaph for Valeria Daca, who died at 18 years of age, intended to be read by passersby and expressing a wish for her rest.

EDCS-08800242.summary: A simple funerary epitaph naming three individuals, likely Marcus Lucius Titus.

EDCS-08800253.translation: Bidbal Tancini, freedman, is buried here.
EDCS-08800253.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Bidbal Tancini, a freedman.

EDCS-08800255.translation: Caius Iulius Aquila, son of Arcon Valbus, is buried here.
EDCS-08800255.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Caius Iulius Aquila, son of Arcon Valbus.

EDCS-08800256.summary: A funerary epitaph recording the burial of Pisanus.

EDCS-08800262.translation: For the memory of Quintus Atilius Stellatinus, son of Quintus, from Ferentis, most beloved son; and of Paullinianus, a cavalry prefect, and Maximilla, his parents.
EDCS-08800262.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by parents, Paullinianus and Maximilla, for their beloved son Quintus Atilius Stellatinus, also mentioning Paullinianus's military rank.

EDCS-08800265.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Germanus, formerly a cavalryman of the Fourth Gallic Cohort, troop of Optatus, having served 17 years, aged 40, from the province of Lusitania, his kinsman Faustinus Laeti, a citizen, had this made.
EDCS-08800265.summary: This funerary inscription records the dedication of a tomb by Faustinus Laeti for Germanus, a cavalryman from Lusitania who served 17 years and was 40 years old.

EDCS-08800270.translation: By decree of the town councillors, the state of Salensium [dedicates this] to the most noble Caesar.
EDCS-08800270.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the municipality of Salensium to Caesar, likely honoring him by decree of its local council.

EDCS-08800272.translation: Herennius Hos is, equestrian prefect, prefect.
EDCS-08800272.summary: This very brief inscription likely records the name and titles of Herennius Hos is, possibly indicating a monument erected in his honor or by him.

EDCS-08800274.translation: For the honor of the office of duovir, Postumius Octavianus, having received [permission] from the most distinguished order of Salensium, gave and dedicated this statue with a shrine and doors, at his own expense, in a place entirely [suitable].
EDCS-08800274.summary: This inscription records Postumius Octavianus dedicating a statue with a shrine and doors at his own expense to commemorate his attainment of the duovirate, approved by the local council of Salensium.

EDCS-08800278.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits, to Gaius Cassius P., aged...
EDCS-08800278.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, likely recording the name and age of Gaius Cassius P.

EDCS-08800279.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Clemen lived for...
EDCS-08800279.summary: This is a fragment of a funerary inscription, recording the name Clemen and the duration of their life.

EDCS-08800282.summary: This inscription is a simple dedication to Marcus Julius Felix, possibly marking his tomb or a monument in his honor.

EDCS-08800283.summary: This inscription records Primigenius, identified as the son of Clementis, likely serving as a basis for identification on a monument.

EDCS-08800303.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Pallas lived for 27 years. Egnatius Saturninus, a stationarius, made this for his most deserving wife.
EDCS-08800303.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Pallas at age 27 and was erected by her husband, Egnatius Saturninus, who was a stationarius.

EDCS-08800307.translation: Sacred to Minerva Augusta. Sextus Julius Epictetus, freedman of Sextus Julius Primigenus, gave this offering for the honor of the office of duovir, and also placed a small bowl according to his vow.
EDCS-08800307.summary: This inscription records Sextus Julius Epictetus, a freedman, dedicating an offering and a small bowl to Minerva Augusta in honor of his holding the office of duovir.

EDCS-08800313.translation: To the genius of the household, Titus Flavius Germanus willingly paid his vow.
EDCS-08800313.summary: This inscription records a vow paid by Titus Flavius Germanus to the genius of his household.

EDCS-08800326.translation: To the unconquered god Mithras, Aurelius Nectoreca, centurion of a detachment of Britons serving in Volubilis, willingly and gladly, as deserved, offered this.
EDCS-08800326.summary: This inscription records a dedication to the god Mithras by Aurelius Nectoreca, a centurion of a detachment of Britons stationed in Volubilis.

EDCS-08800327.translation: Sacred to the god Saturn. A silver statue for Claudia Procula, a most honorable woman, from her will.
EDCS-08800327.summary: This inscription records the dedication of a silver statue to the god Saturn, bequeathed by Claudia Procula.

EDCS-08800329.translation: Sacred to Venus Augusta. Lucius Caecilius Vitalis, freedman of the Caecilii, dedicated and consecrated this statue from his own funds in honor of his seviriate, by decree of the council.
EDCS-08800329.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Venus Augusta by Lucius Caecilius Vitalis, a freedman, in honor of his position as a seviri.

EDCS-08800337.translation: The council of Volubilis decreed that a statue be erected to Marcus Annio Maturo, son of Caius Annius Rufus, aged 22, of the public equestrian order, because of the merits of his parents and his own exceptional character; Claudia Polionilla, his most dutiful mother, erected it with her own funds.
EDCS-08800337.summary: This inscription records the decree by the council of Volubilis to erect a statue for Marcus Annio Maturo, and its erection by his mother, Claudia Polionilla.

EDCS-08800371.translation: For Sufronia Valentina, Clementi's wife, his most perfect husband, the governor and our patron, the public administration of Volubilis erected this by decree of the council.
EDCS-08800371.summary: This inscription records the erection of a monument by the public administration of Volubilis for Sufronia Valentina, wife of the governor Clementi.

EDCS-08800385.translation: To the procurator of the Emperor.
EDCS-08800385.summary: This is a fragment likely referring to an official known as the procurator Augusti.

EDCS-08800387.translation: To Ibzatha, son of Marcus, of the Quirina tribe, aged 17, designated for the public equestrian order, Cornelia Amma erected this for her brother.
EDCS-08800387.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Cornelia Amma for her brother Ibzatha, who was destined for the public equestrian order.

EDCS-08800388.translation: Titus Ocratius Valerianus, a most distinguished man, quaestor pro praetore of the province of Baetica, erected this for his brother because of his meritorious and dutiful conduct towards him.
EDCS-08800388.summary: This inscription records a monument erected by Titus Ocratius Valerianus, a quaestor pro praetore of Baetica, for his brother.

EDCS-08800390.translation: To the illustrious man, an eques Romanus.
EDCS-08800390.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription likely honoring an eques Romanus.

EDCS-08800392.translation: To Antonius, decurion of the municipality of Volubilis, aedile, aged 24 years. Lucius Antonius Ianuarius, his father, erected [this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800392.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius, a young decurion and aedile of Volubilis, erected by his father.

EDCS-08800393.translation: To Lucius Antonius Lucianus, decurion of the municipality of Volubilis, aged 24 years. Lucius Antonius Ianuarius, his father, erected [this] for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800393.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Antonius Lucianus, a young decurion of Volubilis, erected by his father.

EDCS-08800394.translation: The order of Volubilis decreed a statue to Lucius Caecilius, son of Claudia, aedile, decurion, for his merits. Lucius Caecilius Rogatus, his son, erected [it] for his most dutiful father, remitting the expense from his own funds.
EDCS-08800394.summary: This inscription records a decree by the town council of Volubilis to erect a statue for Lucius Caecilius, aedile and decurion, and its subsequent erection by his son.

EDCS-08800395.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, son of Lucius, aedile, twice chief magistrate, and flamen of the municipality. Manlia Romana, daughter-in-law, erected [this] for her most dutiful father-in-law.
EDCS-08800395.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, aedile, duovir, and flamen of the municipality, erected by his daughter-in-law.

EDCS-08800396.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Clemens, son of Lucius, decurion, twice chief magistrate, aged 33 years. Caecilia Caeciliana erected [this] for her dearest son.
EDCS-08800396.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Caecilius Clemens, a young decurion and duovir, erected by his mother.

EDCS-08800397.translation: To Quintus Caecilius Platon, son of Quintus, of the Galeria tribe, aedile, twice chief magistrate, and flamen of the municipality of Volubilis. Caecilia Caeciliana, his daughter, dedicated [this] to her most dutiful father.
EDCS-08800397.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Quintus Caecilius Platon, aedile, duovir, and flamen of Volubilis, dedicated by his daughter.

EDCS-08800398.translation: To Fabia Bira, daughter of Izeltas, first flaminica in the municipality of Volubilis, most dutiful and well-deserving towards her own people. Marcus Valerius Antiochus, freedman of Severus, dedicated and consecrated [this] from his own funds.
EDCS-08800398.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Fabia Bira, a prominent religious official in Volubilis, made by her freedman.

EDCS-08800399.translation: To Fabia Bira, daughter of Izeltas, first flaminica in the municipality of Volubilis. Fabius Crispus, Caecilianus, and Rogatus, son of Crispus, gave [this] for their most indulgent aunt from their own funds.
EDCS-08800399.summary: This inscription records a joint gift to Fabia Bira, first flaminica of Volubilis, made by her nephews.

EDCS-08800400.translation: To Gaius Gellius Senecae, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, decurion of his municipality, aged 18 years. Quintus Gellius Gellianus, his father, and Caecilia Dideia, his mother, erected [this] for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800400.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gaius Gellius Senecae, a young decurion of Volubilis, erected by his parents.

EDCS-08800401.translation: To Marcus Valerius Honoratus, son of Tuscus, decurion, aedile, twice chief magistrate. Valerius Tuscus, his son, erected [this] for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-08800401.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcus Valerius Honoratus, a decurion and duovir, erected by his son.

EDCS-08800402.translation: Marcus Valerius Honoratus, son of Tuscus, grandson of Honoratus, aged 18. His father Tuscus placed this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800402.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a young man named Marcus Valerius Honoratus, set up by his father.

EDCS-08800406.translation: Caecilia Modesta, his mother, and Caecilius Modestus, his brother, placed this at their own expense for Lucius Caecilius Silvanus, son of Lucius, a cavalryman of the Augustan ala.
EDCS-08800406.summary: This funerary monument was erected by the mother and brother for Lucius Caecilius Silvanus, a cavalryman.

EDCS-08800407.summary: This is a dedication by Sextus Antonius Valerianus, possibly to a woman or a place named Antoniana.

EDCS-08800408.translation: For Lucius Antonius Nervae, aged 25, of the municipality.
EDCS-08800408.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Lucius Antonius Nervae, who died at age 25.

EDCS-08800410.translation: Titus Ocratius Valerianus, a most distinguished man, and Quintus Ocratius Titianus, an eminent man, placed this for their brother Gaius Caecilius Flaccus, son of Lucius, aged 25.
EDCS-08800410.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by two prominent men for their brother, Gaius Caecilius Flaccus.

EDCS-08800411.translation: Caecilia Caeciliana, using the privilege of the highest reverence, placed this statue and provided for the expenses of the funeral for Lucius Caecilius Fronton, son of Lucius, of Volubilis, who died in Rome, aged 25, for whom the council of Volubilis decreed a statue and funeral expenses due to his merits and his parents' merits and his own disposition.
EDCS-08800411.summary: This monument honors Lucius Caecilius Fronton, a young man from Volubilis who died in Rome, with a statue and funeral expenses decreed by the local council and funded by Caecilia Caeciliana.

EDCS-08800412.translation: Marcus Caecilius Fuscinus, his brother, and Aemilia Honorata, his mother, placed this for their most dutiful son, Lucius Caecilius Gellianus, son of Lucius.
EDCS-08800412.summary: This funerary epitaph was erected by the brother and mother for Lucius Caecilius Gellianus.

EDCS-08800413.translation: For Lucius Caecilius Kassianus, son of Lucius, the council of the municipality of Volubilis for his merits and those of his parents.
EDCS-08800413.summary: The council of Volubilis honored Lucius Caecilius Kassianus for his merits and those of his parents.

EDCS-08800414.translation: Caecilius Celsinus and Valeria Gaetula, parents, gave and dedicated this to their most dutiful son, Lucius Caecilius Platon, son of Lucius, at their own expense.
EDCS-08800414.summary: Parents Caecilius Celsinus and Valeria Gaetula dedicated this funerary monument to their son, Lucius Caecilius Platon.

EDCS-08800416.translation: Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, his father, and Valeria Manlia, his mother, gave this for their most dutiful daughter, Caecilia Caeciliana, at their own expense.
EDCS-08800416.summary: Parents Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus and Valeria Manlia erected this funerary monument for their daughter, Caecilia Caeciliana.

EDCS-08800417.translation: To Marcus Claudius Germanus Volubilitanus, son of Quintus, aged 8 years. Quintus Claudius Saturninus and Flavia Germanilla set this up for their dearest son.
EDCS-08800417.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for an 8-year-old boy, set up by his parents, likely from Volubilis, and dated to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800418.translation: To Lucius Fabius Romanus, son of Lucius. Fabii Manlianus and Saturninus, brothers, set this up.
EDCS-08800418.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucius Fabius Romanus, set up by his brothers, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800419.translation: To Mamilia Lucilla, daughter of Caius, from Baetica, of the municipality of Conobaria, aged 33 years and 16 days. Lucius Valerius Saturninus set this up for his most deserving wife, remitting the expense of the funeral and the statue, which the council of Volubilitani also decreed at their own expense.
EDCS-08800419.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mamilia Lucilla, set up by her husband Lucius Valerius Saturninus, who also covered the costs of a statue decreed by the local council, from Volubilis, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800420.translation: Aged 16 years. Manlia Romana set this up for her most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800420.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a 16-year-old boy, set up by his mother Manlia Romana, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800421.translation: To Lucius Sariolenus Proculus, son of Lucius, of the Quirina tribe, patron of Volubilitani, by decree of the decurions.
EDCS-08800421.summary: This inscription honors Lucius Sariolenus Proculus, a patron of Volubilis, by decree of the local council, likely Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800422.translation: To Valeria Caeciliana, daughter of Tuscus, wife of Sextus Valerius Priscus. Valerius Tuscus, father, set this up for his most indulgent daughter, [in honor of] Va Max, the council of Volubilitani, and her husbands.
EDCS-08800422.summary: This funerary epitaph for Valeria Caeciliana was set up by her father, with honors also from the council of Volubilitani and her husbands, likely from Volubilis, Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800424.translation: To Valeria Gelliana, daughter of Quintus. Quintus Valerius Masculus and Gabinia Anulla, parents.
EDCS-08800424.summary: This funerary epitaph for Valeria Gelliana was set up by her parents, Quintus Valerius Masculus and Gabinia Anulla, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800425.translation: To Valeria Myggyn, daughter of Pudentis. Marcus Valerius Sassius Pudens set this up for his most dutiful sister at his own expense.
EDCS-08800425.summary: This funerary epitaph for Valeria Myggyn was set up by her brother Marcus Valerius Sassius Pudens, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800426.translation: To Lucius Valerius Priscus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, aged 17 years. Marcus Valerius Peregrinus, father, set this up for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800426.summary: This funerary epitaph for Lucius Valerius Priscus, aged 17, was set up by his father Marcus Valerius Peregrinus, likely from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800427.translation: Aged 8 years and 38 days. Lucius Valerius Saturninus and Valeria Caeciliana set this up for their son, remitting the expense of the funeral and the statue, which the council of Volubilitani also decreed at their own expense.
EDCS-08800427.summary: This funerary epitaph for a son aged 8 years and 38 days was set up by his parents Lucius Valerius Saturninus and Valeria Caeciliana, with costs for the funeral and a statue covered by the Volubilitani council, likely from Volubilis, Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800428.translation: [His parents] and V...ana set this up for their most devoted son.
EDCS-08800428.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by parents for their son.

EDCS-08800441.translation: Paternus and Paternus's daughter and Severus's son Maurus Turradus with his wife Iulia Valeria and his son Valerius Flavius and daughter Valeria Pusinca, Terentius Capito with his wife Maura Pullut and his son M. Terentius Passerus and his son Terentius Honoratus, R...us with ENI... Germanus...
EDCS-08800441.summary: This incomplete inscription lists multiple individuals, likely family members, possibly for a funerary monument or a dedication.

EDCS-08800445.translation: The names of veterans and Roman citizens and local leaders: Aemilius Maternus, Valerius Flavus, Sentius Saturninus, Claudius Fuscinus, Calvius Seneca, Aemilius Rufus, Pompeius Capito, Valerius Rogatus, Fabius Marinus, Helvius Maternus, Valerius Arrenus, Mart. C. Valerius Quadratus, Fabius Ianuarius, Valerius Valens, son of Bufuma, Sentius Bargbal, Valerius Ianuarius, Valerius.
EDCS-08800445.summary: This inscription lists the names of veterans, Roman citizens, and local leaders, possibly for a public record or honor.

EDCS-08800446.translation: ...junior, Iuvenalis, S. Fuscus, Peregrinus, Fortunatus, s. Fuscus, s. Arquius, N... of Capito, A. Flavinus, A. Servatus, RV Veteranus the elder, IV Flavus, CA RE, Antonus, Iul[ius?].
EDCS-08800446.summary: This is a fragmented inscription listing several personal names, possibly related to a funerary monument or a list of officials.

EDCS-08800447.translation: On the fifteenth day before the Kalends of August, Po. Ingenuus, V. Gallus, PL. Ieius...
EDCS-08800447.summary: This is an incomplete inscription recording a date and personal names, possibly related to an event or a dedication.

EDCS-08800450.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Aemilia Urbana and Narcissus made this for Marcus Aemilius Severus, flamen of the municipality of Volubilis, decurion, duovir, aged 57, and for their patron.
EDCS-08800450.summary: This funerary inscription is dedicated to Marcus Aemilius Severus, a local magistrate in Volubilis, by his wife and another individual who appears to be his patron, likely from the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08800451.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Flavia Germanilla, daughter of Titus, of Volubilis, flaminica of the province, lived 72 years and 6 months.
EDCS-08800451.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Flavia Germanilla, a provincial priestess from Volubilis, likely dating to the 2nd or 3rd century AD.

EDCS-08800452.translation: In memory of Iulius...vice, the overseer, for whom his parents and grandchildren made an eternal home. He lived 18 years and departed on the Kalends of November in the provincial year 566.
EDCS-08800452.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Iulius vice at the age of 18, with the provincial year 566 likely corresponding to 495 AD.

EDCS-08800453.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Caius Iunius Iu Senior, quaestor, duovir, flamen, lived 78 years.
EDCS-08800453.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Caius Iunius Iu Senior, who held several local magistracies, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800454.translation: Cosuobnus Priscus, son of Tatirus, an Aeduan, an *eques* of the ala Tauriana, aged 45, lies here. May the earth be light upon you. He spent 24 *aeru*s. The heir by the will had this made.
EDCS-08800454.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Cosuobnus Priscus, an Aeduan cavalryman in the ala Tauriana, who died at 45, with his heir arranging the burial and specifying a sum of money, likely dating to the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800456.translation: Saturninus, son of Fabricius, centurion of the 1st Cohort of Spaniards, lies here. Niger dedicated [this].
EDCS-08800456.summary: A tombstone recording the burial of Saturninus, a centurion, and mentioning Niger as the dedicator.

EDCS-08800457.translation: Marcus Valerius Tolosa Rufinus, soldier of the 10th Gemina Legion, centuria Ati, aged 30 years and 11 months, lies here. May the earth be light for you. His heir Secundus made this.
EDCS-08800457.summary: A tombstone for Marcus Valerius Tolosa Rufinus, a soldier of the 10th Gemina Legion, erected by his heir Secundus.

EDCS-08800459.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aurelius Philippus Syrus, a merchant, son of Aurelius Samitus Maximus, lived 40 years. His most devoted brothers placed this.
EDCS-08800459.summary: A tombstone for Aurelius Philippus Syrus, a merchant, erected by his brothers.

EDCS-08800462.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aemilius Iulianus lived 35 years.
EDCS-08800462.summary: A tombstone recording the life span of Aemilius Iulianus.

EDCS-08800463.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Aleia Metricia lived 35 years, 10 months, and 5 days. Her daughter Aleia Donata made this for her mother.
EDCS-08800463.summary: A tombstone for Aleia Metricia, erected by her daughter Aleia Donata.

EDCS-08800465.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Anna, deserving [of remembrance], lived 100 years.
EDCS-08800465.summary: A tombstone recording the life span of Anna, who lived to be 100 years old.

EDCS-08800466.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Annonia Severa lived 18 years. Her beloved mother made this.
EDCS-08800466.summary: A tombstone for Annonia Severa, erected by her mother.

EDCS-08800467.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Antoniana.
EDCS-08800467.summary: A tombstone inscription that only records the name Antoniana.

EDCS-08800468.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Iustus and Secundus, sons, placed [this] for Marcus Antonius Antonianus and Caecilia Saturnina.
EDCS-08800468.summary: A tombstone erected by Iustus and Secundus for Marcus Antonius Antonianus and Caecilia Saturnina.

EDCS-08800469.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Antonius Balbus Vitalis lived [duration unspecified, but] 7 months. Antonia Modesta fulfilled the vow for her husband from her will, with a willing spirit.
EDCS-08800469.summary: A tombstone inscription that records the death of Antonius Balbus Vitalis and states that his wife Antonia Modesta fulfilled a vow from his will.

EDCS-08800470.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonius Sabbatarus lived for 3 years, 5 months, and 4 days. His most devoted father made this.
EDCS-08800470.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius Sabbatarus, erected by his father.

EDCS-08800471.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonius Valerianus lived for 34 years. His most devoted sisters placed this.
EDCS-08800471.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonius Valerianus, erected by his sisters.

EDCS-08800472.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Ceciliana lived for 29 years and 5 months. Antonius Fabianus made this for his sister.
EDCS-08800472.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Ceciliana, erected by her brother Antonius Fabianus.

EDCS-08800473.translation: Caecilia placed this for Antonia Felicitas.
EDCS-08800473.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Caecilia for Antonia Felicitas.

EDCS-08800474.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Antonia Caia lived for 45 years. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-08800474.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antonia Caia, including a common wish for the deceased.

EDCS-08800476.translation: Antoni AN NI [lived for] one year.
EDCS-08800476.summary: This inscription records the age of a child named Antoni, likely an epitaph.

EDCS-08800477.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Caius Apronius Paternus Bracarus lived for 18 years, 10 months, and 28 days. He is buried here. Priamus made this mausoleum for him. You who read this, I ask that you say, 'May the earth lie lightly on you, for life was short.'
EDCS-08800477.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caius Apronius Paternus Bracarus, erected by Priamus, who also composed a plea to the reader.

EDCS-08800479.translation: Of the Avinii. Lived 18 years. Fuscianus made this for himself.
EDCS-08800479.summary: This funerary inscription records that Fuscianus, possibly related to the Avinii family, made this tomb for himself.

EDCS-08800480.translation: Lived for 10 years, 7 months, and 20 days. Aurelius the father placed this.
EDCS-08800480.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for a child, erected by their father, Aurelius.

EDCS-08800481.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Departed. Marcia, [mother/daughter] of Aurelia Aethala, lived for 11 months and 1 day. Her grandson Aurelius Summus Vitalis, also called Sabinus, and his father made this.
EDCS-08800481.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Aurelia Aethala, erected by her grandson and great-grandson.

EDCS-08800482.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurelia Audia Alexandria Syra Phoenix lived 26 years.
EDCS-08800482.summary: A funerary dedication for Aurelia Audia Alexandria Syra Phoenix, who lived 26 years.

EDCS-08800483.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Aurellia Syra lived 20 years and 5 days. Her most devoted mother, Aurellia, placed this.
EDCS-08800483.summary: A funerary dedication by Aurellia for her daughter Aurellia Syra, who lived 20 years and 5 days.

EDCS-08800485.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Bolanius Sabinus Syrofoenix lived 55 years and is buried here. May the earth lie lightly upon you. Ausius Ausonius, his cousin, made this.
EDCS-08800485.summary: A funerary dedication by Ausius Ausonius for his cousin Bolanius Sabinus Syrofoenix, who lived 55 years.

EDCS-08800486.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Domitia Ia placed this for her most devoted husband, Caeciliano, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-08800486.summary: A funerary dedication by Domitia Ia for her husband Caeciliano, who lived 27 years.

EDCS-08800487.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilia Musa lies here, most unhappy. She lived 40 years. Caecilia Euterpe placed this for her patroness.
EDCS-08800487.summary: A funerary dedication by Caecilia Euterpe for her patroness Caecilia Musa, who lived 40 years.

EDCS-08800489.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. To Marcus Caecilius Ibzathas.
EDCS-08800489.summary: A funerary dedication to Marcus Caecilius Ibzathas.

EDCS-08800490.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Marcus Caecilius Lucanus Caecilianus lived 43 years, 10 months, and 28 days. Marcus Caecilius Caecilianus and Marcus Caecilius Pudens, his sons, placed this.
EDCS-08800490.summary: A funerary dedication by the sons Marcus Caecilius Caecilianus and Marcus Caecilius Pudens for their father, Marcus Caecilius Lucanus Caecilianus, who lived 43 years, 10 months, and 28 days.

EDCS-08800491.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [Name] son of Lucius Caecilius lived 33 years, 2 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-08800491.summary: A funerary dedication for a son of Lucius Caecilius, who lived 33 years, 2 months, and 10 days.

EDCS-08800493.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Caecilius.
EDCS-08800493.summary: A fragmentary funerary dedication for someone named Caecilius.

EDCS-08800494.translation: For the most beloved Caecilius. [Name] placed this. He lived 25 years.
EDCS-08800494.summary: A funerary dedication for the beloved Caecilius, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-08800495.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Caecilia IIN MIIII, Caecilius II IAIA, husband [dedicated this] to his wife.
EDCS-08800495.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Caecilia IIN MIIII, dedicated by her husband Caecilius II IAIA.

EDCS-08800496.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Caecilia Antoniana, his most dutiful wife, aged 45, Caius Julius Severianus, her husband, placed this.
EDCS-08800496.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication to Caecilia Antoniana, aged 45, by her husband Caius Julius Severianus.

EDCS-08800497.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Primus, her husband, and Caecilius Antinous, his dutiful patron, made this for his wife Caecilia Dativa, who lived 18 years.
EDCS-08800497.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Caecilia Dativa, who lived 18 years, dedicated by her husband Primus and her patron Caecilius Antinous.

EDCS-08800498.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Caecilia Pompeiana, aged 15, Pompeius Macedo placed this with his mother for his most dutiful daughter.
EDCS-08800498.summary: This funerary epitaph is for Caecilia Pompeiana, aged 15, placed by her father Pompeius Macedo and mother.

EDCS-08800500.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cassius Botto lived 30 years.
EDCS-08800500.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Cassius Botto, who lived 30 years.

EDCS-08800501.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Cassius Comitus, who lived 60 years.
EDCS-08800501.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Cassius Comitus, who lived 60 years.

EDCS-08800502.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. To Kassis Macedonis.
EDCS-08800502.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Kassis Macedonis.

EDCS-08800503.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Castricius Bubulcus lived 25 years.
EDCS-08800503.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life and death of Castricius Bubulcus, who lived 25 years.

EDCS-08800505.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Claudia Helena lived 62 years. Cassius Felix made this.
EDCS-08800505.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Claudia Helena, aged 62, and was made by Cassius Felix.

EDCS-08800506.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Cogitatus placed this for his son Dorgellus, aged 3.
EDCS-08800506.summary: This funerary epitaph records the dedication by Cogitatus for his son Dorgellus, who died at age 3.

EDCS-08800507.translation: To the divine shades. Crispus the father and V.RA the mother placed [this] for Cogitata; she lived 15 years.
EDCS-08800507.summary: This funerary inscription records the memorial set up by parents for their deceased daughter Cogitata.

EDCS-08800509.translation: To the divine shades. Salutius Syrophoenix made this funeral arrangement and honorable obsequies for his incomparable wife Syraphoenix, and also this burial place.
EDCS-08800509.summary: This funerary inscription records a husband's arrangements and provision of a burial place for his wife Syraphoenix.

EDCS-08800511.translation: To the divine shades, a sacred offering. His sons placed [this] for Cassius Felix, their good father.
EDCS-08800511.summary: This funerary inscription records a memorial set up by sons for their deceased father Cassius Felix.

EDCS-08800512.translation: To the divine shades, a sacred offering. Urbana placed [this] for her husband C. Felicisimus, aged 31 years and 7 days.
EDCS-08800512.summary: This funerary inscription records a wife's memorial for her husband C. Felicisimus.

EDCS-08800513.translation: To the divine shades. Publius Flavius Nicocus [dedicated this] for himself and his family.
EDCS-08800513.summary: This funerary inscription records a man named Publius Flavius Nicocus dedicating a memorial for himself and his relatives.

EDCS-08800514.translation: To the divine shades, a sacred offering. Flavius Praefectus lived 58 years, 8 months, and 14 days.
EDCS-08800514.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Flavius Praefectus.

EDCS-08800515.summary: This is a simple funerary inscription for Quintus Florius Florinus.

EDCS-08800516.translation: To the divine shades, a sacred offering. For Gabinia Antoniana; she lived 23 years, 6 months, and 27 days.
EDCS-08800516.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Gabinia Antoniana.

EDCS-08800517.summary: This funerary inscription records the lifespan of Gabinia Compitaria.

EDCS-08800519.translation: Helvia Sacci, the mother, placed [this] for her son Subutanus, aged 18 years.
EDCS-08800519.summary: This funerary inscription records a mother's memorial for her son Subutanus.

EDCS-08800520.translation: Icia Ra and Honorata, her daughter, and her husband, and Rata to their parents.
EDCS-08800520.summary: This funerary inscription records a dedication made by family members to their parents, likely from the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-08800521.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Kassius Ianuarius made this for his most devoted wife, Cogitata.
EDCS-08800521.summary: This is a funerary dedication made by Kassius Ianuarius for his wife Cogitata, likely dating to the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-08800522.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Iaps, his brother.
EDCS-08800522.summary: This funerary dedication is for Iaps, made by an unnamed brother, likely dating to the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-08800523.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Iulius Cogitatus, his son, aged 4 years.
EDCS-08800523.summary: This funerary dedication is for Iulius Cogitatus, aged four, made by an unnamed father, likely dating to the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-08800524.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Caius Iunius Maximus lived 5 years and 10 days. Antonia Maximina set this up for her son.
EDCS-08800524.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Caius Iunius Maximus at age 5 and 10 days, with his mother Antonia Maximina as the dedicator.

EDCS-08800525.translation: In memory of Iulius Principius, for whom his sons made an eternal home. He lived about 64 years, departed in peace on the 7th day before the Kalends of August in the 566th year of the province.
EDCS-08800525.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulius Principius, with his sons as dedicators, and provides a precise date of death within the provincial era, likely from the 3rd or 4th century AD.

EDCS-08800526.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Of Lucius Iulius Victor.
EDCS-08800526.summary: This is a brief funerary dedication for Lucius Iulius Victor, likely dating to the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-08800528.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Nonna lived 7 years. She rests here. May the earth be light for you.
EDCS-08800528.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Nonna at age 7, with a wish for a light earth, likely from the later Roman Empire.

EDCS-08800529.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. In memory of Iulia Rogativa from Altava, a captive, for whom her sons and grandsons made [this]. She lived about 76 years, departed in peace in the 616th year of the province.
EDCS-08800529.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Rogativa from Altava, with her descendants as dedicators, and provides a date within the provincial era, likely from the 3rd or 4th century AD.

EDCS-08800530.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Iulia Severa lived 17 years, 8 months, and 13 days. Iulius Secundus, her most unhappy father, set this up.
EDCS-08800530.summary: This funerary inscription records the death of Iulia Severa at age 17 years, 8 months, and 13 days, with her grieving father Iulius Secundus as the dedicator.

EDCS-08800532.summary: This is a fragmented inscription identifying a person named Lacaetanus, likely a dedication from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800533.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lepidia Zinit placed this for Lepidius Felix, her companion, who lived 27 years.
EDCS-08800533.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lepidius Felix, placed by his partner Lepidia Zinit, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800535.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucretius Porcelus lived 70 years, 5 months, and 5 days. Claudia Thetis made this for her well-deserving husband.
EDCS-08800535.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Lucretius Porcelus, erected by his wife Claudia Thetis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800536.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Lucretius Navco M. lived 80 years.
EDCS-08800536.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph for Lucretius Navco M., dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800537.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Valeria Valeriana lived 53 years. Quadratinus, her husband, placed this.
EDCS-08800537.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valeria Valeriana, erected by her husband Quadratinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800538.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Mallia, the mother, placed this for Urbana, her daughter, who lived 8 years.
EDCS-08800538.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Urbana, erected by her mother Mallia, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800539.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Marcia Aug lived 42 years, 1 month, and 16 days. Valerius Nonnus, her husband, made this memorial.
EDCS-08800539.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Marcia Aug, erected by her husband Valerius Nonnus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800540.translation: In memory of Vanatius Maternus, for whom his sons and mother made an eternal home; he lived more or less and departed in peace in the 560th year of the province.
EDCS-08800540.summary: This funerary inscription commemorates Vanatius Maternus, erected by his family and dating to the provincial era, likely 560 AD.

EDCS-08800541.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maurus lived 34 years.
EDCS-08800541.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Maurus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800542.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the underworld. Maxi[mus?]...
EDCS-08800542.summary: This is a fragmented funerary epitaph, possibly for a person named Maximus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800543.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Caecilius Maximus lived eighty years. His granddaughters Antonia and Maximina erected this.
EDCS-08800543.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caecilius Maximus, erected by his granddaughters.

EDCS-08800544.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Ronius Modestus lived 47 years. His wife and sons erected this.
EDCS-08800544.summary: A funerary epitaph for Ronius Modestus, erected by his wife and sons.

EDCS-08800545.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Lucius Ocratius Senatus lived 30 years, 5 months, and 15 days. Iulia Suavilla made this for her beloved husband.
EDCS-08800545.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Ocratius Senatus, erected by his wife Iulia Suavilla.

EDCS-08800546.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Octavius Restutus lived 30 years. Aurelia Aelia placed this for her most excellent husband.
EDCS-08800546.summary: A funerary epitaph for Octavius Restutus, erected by his wife Aurelia Aelia.

EDCS-08800547.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Petronia Ianuaria lived 47 years, [some number of] months, [some number of] days.
EDCS-08800547.summary: A funerary epitaph for Petronia Ianuaria, recording her age in years, months, and days.

EDCS-08800548.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Pompeius Attalius lived 61 years.
EDCS-08800548.summary: A funerary epitaph for Pompeius Attalius, recording his age.

EDCS-08800549.summary: A dedication made by sons for an unnamed individual.

EDCS-08800550.translation: Possidianus and [---] and [---] erected this for a most distinguished man, their patron.
EDCS-08800550.summary: A dedication by freedmen (or clients) to their patron.

EDCS-08800553.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Renex, the mother, made this for her son Maral from her own resources.
EDCS-08800553.summary: A funerary epitaph for Maral, erected by his mother Renex.

EDCS-08800554.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Restutus lived 8 years. His father made this for his son.
EDCS-08800554.summary: A funerary epitaph for Restutus, erected by his father.

EDCS-08800555.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Numtius Sabinianus lived X years. Sabinus, his father, [erected this] for his most devoted son.
EDCS-08800555.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Numtius Sabinianus, set up by his father, Sabinus.

EDCS-08800556.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Secundus, son of P. Ma. L., devoted, lived 7 years.
EDCS-08800556.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Secundus, who died at the age of seven.

EDCS-08800558.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sempronius Primus of Sitifis, most devotedly [erected this] for his daughter Sempronia Prima, aged 15. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-08800558.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Sempronius Primus to his daughter Sempronia Prima.

EDCS-08800559.translation: To Mucana, daughter of Publius Serti.
EDCS-08800559.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Mucana, daughter of Publius Serti.

EDCS-08800562.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Titinia Fortunata, freedwoman of Caius.
EDCS-08800562.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Titinia Fortunata, a freedwoman.

EDCS-08800563.translation: To the most devoted son, Ruseucius, aged two, and Victor. DISLVS II RI inia et ANIS ANIS OATOR Victor lived TATVS Servilius
EDCS-08800563.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Ruseucius and Victor, dedicated by Servilius.

EDCS-08800564.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. V. Titulla lived 50 years.
EDCS-08800564.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for V. Titulla, who died at the age of 50.

EDCS-08800566.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. For Valerius Antonianus, aged 5.
EDCS-08800566.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Antonianus, who died at the age of five.

EDCS-08800567.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Kassius Suetus lived 50 years and 15 days. Caius Kassius Valerius Balbus dedicated [this] to his freedman.
EDCS-08800567.summary: This is a funerary epitaph dedicated by Caius Kassius Valerius Balbus to his freedman, Kassius Suetus.

EDCS-08800568.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Caecilianus lived 18 years and 7 months. His father placed [this].
EDCS-08800568.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Valerius Caecilianus, erected by his father.

EDCS-08800569.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Lucius Valerius Caecilianus set this up for his dutiful son Lucius Valerius Quadratus, who lived 6 years, 10 months, and 10 days.
EDCS-08800569.summary: A funerary dedication by Lucius Valerius Caecilianus for his son Lucius Valerius Quadratus.

EDCS-08800570.translation: Valerius Cerialis lived 70 years. Valerius set this up.
EDCS-08800570.summary: A funerary monument erected by Valerius for Valerius Cerialis.

EDCS-08800571.translation: His dutiful son made this for Valerius.
EDCS-08800571.summary: A dedication by an unnamed son for Valerius.

EDCS-08800572.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Valerius Aeventius lived 30 years. His mother Valeria Dativa set this up for her son.
EDCS-08800572.summary: A funerary dedication by Valeria Dativa for her son Valerius Aeventius.

EDCS-08800573.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Valerius Honoratus lived 12 years. Valerius Paulinus set this up.
EDCS-08800573.summary: A funerary dedication by Valerius Paulinus for Valerius Honoratus.

EDCS-08800574.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Gaius Valerius Modestus lived 65 years, 6 months, and 10 days. His dutiful son Gaius Valerius Antonianus set this up for his most dutiful father.
EDCS-08800574.summary: A funerary dedication by Gaius Valerius Antonianus for his father Gaius Valerius Modestus.

EDCS-08800575.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Valerius Paulinus lived 23 years and 2 months. His grandfather set this up.
EDCS-08800575.summary: A funerary dedication by the grandfather of Valerius Paulinus.

EDCS-08800576.translation: To the gods of the underworld. Valerius Romanus lived 25 years. Cecilia Iulia set this up for her husband.
EDCS-08800576.summary: A funerary dedication by Cecilia Iulia for her husband Valerius Romanus.

EDCS-08800578.summary: A simple marker for Valeria Agate, possibly a gravestone or dedication, with a Greek name 'Tyche' (Fortune).

EDCS-08800580.translation: Valeria Felicitas lived 9 years, 3 months. Caecilia Severa set up this monument for herself, buried here.
EDCS-08800580.summary: A funerary monument set up by Caecilia Severa for herself, commemorating her daughter Valeria Felicitas.

EDCS-08800581.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Iulia lived 24 years.
EDCS-08800581.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the death of Valeria Iulia at the age of 24.

EDCS-08800582.translation: To Valeria Lucana, daughter of Lucanus.
EDCS-08800582.summary: This is a funerary dedication for Valeria Lucana, identifying her parentage.

EDCS-08800584.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Her husband made this for Valeria Parthenope, aged 17.
EDCS-08800584.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Valeria Parthenope at age 17 and identifies her husband as the dedicator.

EDCS-08800585.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Valeria Proclinia, aged 9. Proclinius made this.
EDCS-08800585.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Valeria Proclinia at age 9 and identifies Proclinius as the dedicator.

EDCS-08800587.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Victoricus lived 15 years and 14 days. His father Victorinus made this for his most dutiful son.
EDCS-08800587.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Victoricus at age 15 and notes that his father, Victorinus, erected the monument.

EDCS-08800588.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Ovius lived 24 years, 8 months.
EDCS-08800588.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of Ovius at the age of 24 years and 8 months.

EDCS-08800646.summary: This is a funerary dedication where an individual identified as INV made the monument for Iulius.

EDCS-08800656.translation: His most dutiful father made this for [---] vixit 17 years.
EDCS-08800656.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of an individual aged 17, whose father erected the monument.

EDCS-08800676.translation: Her devoted husband placed this for his wife, aged 23.
EDCS-08800676.summary: This funerary epitaph records the death of a wife at age 23, with her devoted husband identified as the dedicator.

EDCS-08800691.translation: For his wife Epagatus made this. Sacred to the Divine Shades.
EDCS-08800691.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Epagatus dedicated this monument to his wife.

EDCS-08800701.summary: This is a fragmentary funerary inscription, possibly naming the deceased Titus.

EDCS-08800709.summary: This inscription records the name of Emperor Marcus Aurelius, likely part of a larger dedication or honorific text.

EDCS-08800710.translation: To the Genius of Ulpius, Lucius Fabius Flaccus, prefect of the cohort of the Parthians, T.
EDCS-08800710.summary: This is a dedication to a Genius, made by Lucius Fabius Flaccus, prefect of a cohort of Parthians, likely from the late 1st or early 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08800712.translation: Annius Afrinus, a sesquiplicarius of the ala Hamiorum, aged 45, is here. Julia Cessia, his wife, and Niger Monimus, a veteran, had this made at their own expense for their well-deserving husband. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08800712.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Annius Afrinus, funded by his wife and a veteran, likely from the 2nd century CE, as indicated by the military titles and the mention of a sesquiplicarius.

EDCS-08800714.translation: To Loucius, an equestrian from Moesia, for his well-deserving Hospita, he made this. May the earth be light upon you.
EDCS-08800714.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Loucius by an equestrian from Moesia, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08800716.translation: To the Frugiferous Holy God, Gaius Julius Longinus, prefect of the cohort of the Astures and Callaeci, fulfilled his vow willingly with a happy heart for his recovered health and well-being.
EDCS-08800716.summary: This is a votive inscription dedicated to a local deity by Gaius Julius Longinus, a military commander, likely dating to the 2nd century CE, recording the fulfillment of a vow for recovery.

EDCS-08800717.translation: To the Nymphs and the Genius of the place, Vallius Maximianus, procurator of the Augusti.
EDCS-08800717.summary: This is a dedication to local deities by Vallius Maximianus, a procurator, likely from the late 2nd or early 3rd century CE.

EDCS-08800722.translation: Aemilius, prefect of the Second Cohort.
EDCS-08800722.summary: This inscription records the name and title of Aemilius, prefect of the Second Cohort, likely part of a larger monument or dedication from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800724.translation: To the Spirits of the Underworld, for Lucius Appius Sabinus, a veteran.
EDCS-08800724.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated to Lucius Appius Sabinus, a veteran, from the Roman imperial period.

EDCS-08800725.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Underworld. Volsienus, a veteran, formerly a decurion of the ala Asturum, lived 40 years. The decurions and optiones of the ala erected this.
EDCS-08800725.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Volsienus, a veteran and former decurion, erected by his colleagues in the ala Asturum, likely from the 2nd century CE.

EDCS-08800726.translation: To the spirits of the departed. Herennia Viia lived 35 years. Her husband Maurus erected this.
EDCS-08800726.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a husband for his deceased wife, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800727.translation: Clara, a devoted daughter, aged 24, and Iulius Clarus, aged 24, and their mother Marcia made this.
EDCS-08800727.summary: A funerary monument erected by children and their mother for deceased family members, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800728.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Sextilia Aemilia, wife of Iulius Crescentis, lived 70 years. Sextilius Honoratus decorated and made this urn for his devoted and most pious mother.
EDCS-08800728.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a son for his deceased mother, who was the wife of Iulius Crescentis, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800729.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. Valerius Silo erected this for his most devoted daughter Valeria Africana, who lived 24 years and 2 months.
EDCS-08800729.summary: A funerary epitaph set up by a father for his deceased daughter, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08800730.translation: Sacred to the spirits of the departed. S Marcellinus lived 7 years.
EDCS-08800730.summary: A funerary epitaph for a young child named S Marcellinus, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-08900734.translation: Claudius Septimus, 25, a distinguished discus-thrower, victor in many contests, dear and accepted by his Emperor, his beloved wife Fausta made this urn for his memory.
EDCS-08900734.summary: A funerary monument erected by a wife for her deceased husband, Claudius Septimus, noted as an athlete and favored by the Emperor, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300635.translation: To Licinia Urbica. From Licinia Urbica.
EDCS-09300635.summary: A funerary dedication possibly from a daughter to her mother, both named Licinia Urbica, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09300639.translation: Of Gaius Vegetius, a baker, a centurion of the veterans.
EDCS-09300639.summary: An inscription on a tombstone or monument for Gaius Vegetius, a veteran centurion and baker, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-09701828.summary: A simple funerary inscription for Publius Hertorius, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10301307.translation: Manlia Romana erected this for her most pious father-in-law, Lucius Caecilius, son of Lucius Caecilianus, formerly aedile, duumvir, and flamen of the municipality.
EDCS-10301307.summary: A funerary dedication by a daughter-in-law for her father-in-law, who held several important civic and religious offices in his municipality, dating to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-10301312.translation: Fabia Bira, daughter of Izelta, the chief priestess, gave and dedicated from her own funds in the municipality of Volubilis.
EDCS-10301312.summary: A dedication of a public building or amenity by Fabia Bira, who held the high priestess office in Volubilis, funded by herself.

EDCS-10301323.summary: The name of an individual, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301326.summary: The name of an individual, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301328.summary: The name of an individual, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301329.summary: The name of an individual, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301331.translation: Lucius Valerius Priscus, son of Lucius Valerius, of the Claudia tribe.
EDCS-10301331.summary: The name of an individual, including his father's name and tribal affiliation, likely for a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301332.summary: An inscription identifying Bidbal as a freedman of Lancinus, probably a tombstone.

EDCS-10301334.summary: The name of an individual, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301336.summary: The name of an individual, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10301342.summary: The name of an individual, likely a tombstone or dedication.

EDCS-10702023.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Lucius Ocratius Senanatus lived 30 years, 5 months, and 15 days. Iulia Suavilla made this for her dear husband.
EDCS-10702023.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Ocratius Senanatus, erected by his wife Iulia Suavilla.

EDCS-11900995.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Aurelius Herma, freedman of Aurelius Natalis, lived 4 years. He is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11900995.summary: A funerary epitaph for Caius Aurelius Herma, a freedman, with a wish for a light burial.

EDCS-11900997.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Pii Rohavi, a veteran of 70 years, and Cadami, a veteran of 80 years, are buried here. May the earth lie lightly on them.
EDCS-11900997.summary: A funerary epitaph for two veterans, Pii Rohavi and Cadami.

EDCS-11900998.translation: Cosconius Calvus is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11900998.summary: A brief funerary epitaph for Cosconius Calvus.

EDCS-11900999.summary: A very brief inscription, likely a name identifying an individual.

EDCS-11901001.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Aurelia Maxima lived 50 years. She is buried here. May the earth lie lightly on you.
EDCS-11901001.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aurelia Maxima, who lived 50 years.

EDCS-11901008.translation: To Jupiter Best and Greatest, Juno Regina, Minerva, Victoria, and the Genius. The equestrian of the V Cohort of Casaces, [name illegible], vowed and set this up.
EDCS-11901008.summary: A dedication to several deities by a cavalryman of the V Cohort of Casaces.

EDCS-11901010.translation: To the Divine Spirits. Caius Cassius, [aged illegible].
EDCS-11901010.summary: A fragmentary funerary inscription mentioning Caius Cassius.

EDCS-11901011.summary: A fragmentary inscription, possibly a name followed by an age or other designation.

EDCS-11901014.translation: For Augustus Alexander, the IV Cohort of Gauls, devoted to their divine majesty, placed this in honor of the excellent man Gaius Iulius Maximus, the governor, and Valerius Salvinianus, the prefect.
EDCS-11901014.summary: A dedication by the IV Cohort of Gauls to Emperor Augustus Alexander and honoring two officials, a governor and a prefect.

EDCS-11901020.summary: This is a dedication to the divine aspect of the emperor's fortune.

EDCS-11901025.translation: The people of Volubilis publicly set this up to the divine Faustina, wife of Emperor Caesar Titus Aelius Hadrianus Antoninus Augustus Pius, father of the fatherland, consul for the third time.
EDCS-11901025.summary: This inscription records a public dedication by the people of Volubilis to Empress Faustina, wife of Antoninus Pius.

EDCS-11901030.translation: The most devoted state of Volubilis, by decree of its council, has set this up to Julia Mamaea Pia Felix Augusta, mother of our Augustus, to her divine spirit.
EDCS-11901030.summary: This inscription is a dedication by the city of Volubilis to Julia Mamaea, mother of Emperor Severus Alexander, honoring her divine spirit.

EDCS-11901031.translation: The state of Volubilis, by decree of its council, has set this up to Emperor Caesar Publius Licinius Gallienus Pius Felix Augustus, pontifex maximus, father of the fatherland, holding tribunician power for the third time, consul for the second time, proconsul.
EDCS-11901031.summary: This inscription records a dedication by the city of Volubilis to Emperor Gallienus, listing his titles and offices.

EDCS-11901040.translation: To Lucius Caecilius Cassianus, son of Lucius, of the Galeria tribe, the council of the municipium of Volubilis [set this up] for his merits and those of his parents.
EDCS-11901040.summary: This inscription records the granting of honors by the council of Volubilis to Lucius Caecilius Cassianus for his merits and those of his family.

EDCS-11901048.translation: Manlia Romana set this up to her most dutiful son, aged sixteen years.
EDCS-11901048.summary: This is a funerary inscription set up by a mother for her sixteen-year-old son.

EDCS-11901052.translation: Sextus Antonius Valerinus set this up to Antonia Dideia.
EDCS-11901052.summary: This is a dedication by Sextus Antonius Valerinus to Antonia Dideia.

EDCS-11901053.translation: The council of Volubilis decreed a statue to Caecilia Valeriana, daughter of Lucius Caecilius Saturninus, for the merits of her family, waiving the expense; her brother Lucius Caecilius Rogatus set [it] up from his own funds for his most dutiful sister.
EDCS-11901053.summary: This inscription records the city of Volubilis decreeing a statue for Caecilia Valeriana, which her brother then funded and erected due to her family's merits.

EDCS-11901056.translation: To Valeria Lucana, daughter of Lucanus.
EDCS-11901056.summary: This is a dedication, likely funerary, to Valeria Lucana.

EDCS-11901060.summary: This is a dedication to an imperial procurator.

EDCS-13302541.translation: A sacred offering to Augusta Victoria, Aurelius D took care to have it made.
EDCS-13302541.summary: This is a dedication to Augusta Victoria, commissioned by Aurelius D.

EDCS-13400224.translation: Lucius Flavius Germanus willingly paid his vow to the Genius of the household.
EDCS-13400224.summary: A votive dedication by Lucius Flavius Germanus to the Genius of his household.

EDCS-13600149.translation: Luxurius Vincentius Cupias, a vigorous youth.
EDCS-13600149.summary: A short inscription likely honoring a person named Luxurius Vincentius Cupias.

EDCS-13900605.translation: The tomb of Gaius Vegetius, the baker.
EDCS-13900605.summary: A funerary inscription for Gaius Vegetius, a baker.

EDCS-15100058.translation: From the province of Mauretania Caesariensis, Tubusuctu.
EDCS-15100058.summary: An inscription likely identifying a location or administrative unit in Mauretania Caesariensis.

EDCS-21000670.translation: To his own daughter and to Manlia Aemiliana, his most dutiful son, and to Julius Severus, his best uncle.
EDCS-21000670.summary: A dedication honoring a daughter, son, and uncle, possibly on a shared monument.

EDCS-21000671.translation: To the Divine Shades, to Fabia, my most devoted wife, within 33 months.
EDCS-21000671.summary: A funerary dedication to Fabia, a devoted wife, with a mention of a 33-month period.

EDCS-21000672.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades, Tuscus lived for 2 years and some months.
EDCS-21000672.summary: A funerary inscription for Tuscus, who lived for 2 years and some months.

EDCS-21000673.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Bellicus Bellicanus, an equestrian soldier from the select guard, lived for X years. His son Claudius Modestus erected this.
EDCS-21000673.summary: A funerary monument erected by Claudius Modestus for his father Bellicus Bellicanus, an equestrian officer, who lived for a certain number of years.

EDCS-24700495.translation: A sacred offering to Augusta Isis, Lucius Caecilius Felix, freedman of Lucius Caecilius Caecilianus, dedicated it from his own funds on the occasion of his holding the office of IIIIvir for the fourth time.
EDCS-24700495.summary: A dedication to Augusta Isis by Lucius Caecilius Felix, a freedman, who funded it during his fourth term as a IIIIvir.

EDCS-24700502.translation: To Pius Felix, greatest Germanicus, greatest with tribunician power 10, from M.I.S.I.E.M.I.T.A.T.E.M. POSN.
EDCS-24700502.summary: This is a fragment of a dedication, likely to an emperor, referencing military titles and tribunician power.

EDCS-24700504.translation: Valeria Prisca placed this for her dearest son, Quintus Caecilius Priscus, son of Quintus, of the Quirina tribe, from Volubilis, honored with the public equestrian rank, aged 17.
EDCS-24700504.summary: This is a funerary epitaph set up by a mother for her son, noting his age, tribal affiliation, and equestrian status.

EDCS-24700505.translation: Titus Ocratius Valerianus, most distinguished man, and Quintus Ocratius Titianus, excellent man, brothers, placed this for Caecilia Romana.
EDCS-24700505.summary: This funerary monument was erected by two brothers in honor of Caecilia Romana.

EDCS-24700506.translation: For the honor, memory, and praise of Marcus Maturius Victorinus, son of Marcus Maturius Victor, excellent man, our governor and patron, the public body of Volubilitani, by decree of the council.
EDCS-24700506.summary: This inscription records a public honor bestowed by the town council of Volubilis on their governor and patron, Marcus Maturius Victorinus.

EDCS-24700508.translation: Valerius Marcus, a veteran decurion of the alae, and Cassia Rogata placed this for their most dutiful son, Marcus Valerius Rogatus, aged 11 years, 8 months, and 13 days.
EDCS-24700508.summary: This epitaph commemorates a young boy, Marcus Valerius Rogatus, and was erected by his father, a veteran decurion, and mother.

EDCS-24700509.translation: Caligatus the husband made this, and LIE.
EDCS-24700509.summary: This is a very fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication made by a husband.

EDCS-24700510.translation: Aemilia Urbana, his wife, and their freedmen Aemilius Narcissus and Gandarus placed this for Marcus Aemilius Severus, son of Lucius, of the Claudia tribe, a flamen, decurion, and duovir of the municipality of Volubilis.
EDCS-24700510.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a wife and freedmen for Marcus Aemilius Severus, a local magistrate in Volubilis.

EDCS-24700511.translation: Manlia Romana, his most indulgent mother, placed this for Lucius Caecilius Clemens, son of Caecilianus, a decurion, most indulgent.
EDCS-24700511.summary: This epitaph was set up by a mother for her son, Lucius Caecilius Clemens, who was a decurion.

EDCS-24700512.translation: The council of Volubilitani decreed a statue for Lucius Caecilius Latro, son of Gallus, and Lucius Caecilius Antonianus, son of Lucius, on account of the merits of their parents; Cassia Caeciliana, most indulgent mother, most dutifully erected this monument at her own expense for her son.
EDCS-24700512.summary: This inscription records a public decree for a statue honoring Lucius Caecilius Latro and Lucius Caecilius Antonianus, with a statue base erected by Cassia Caeciliana.

EDCS-24700513.translation: Gabinia Babbus placed this for her most indulgent father, Marcus Gabinius Gallianus, son of Aulus, a decurion and flamen of the municipality of Volubilis.
EDCS-24700513.summary: This funerary monument was erected by a daughter for her father, Marcus Gabinius Gallianus, a local official in Volubilis.

EDCS-24700515.translation: Lucius Pompeius Manlianus, son of Lucius, erected this at his own expense to his brother, Marco Pompeius Antonianus Volubilitanus, son of Lucius, a decurion of his town, who was most affectionate and had deserved excellently of him.
EDCS-24700515.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a brother to his deceased brother, Marco Pompeius Antonianus Volubilitanus, a decurion, likely in the Roman province of Mauretania Tingitana.

EDCS-24700517.translation: Pompeius Antonianus and Manlianus, sons, erected this at their own expense to Fabia Manliana, daughter of Lucius, of Volubilis, wife of Pompeius Senioris.
EDCS-24700517.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by sons to their mother, Fabia Manliana, wife of Pompeius Senioris, from Volubilis.

EDCS-24700518.translation: The public body of Volubilitans, by decree of the council, erected this to Valerius Concordius, son of Valerius Marcellinus, formerly prefect and our patron.
EDCS-24700518.summary: This is a public dedication by the city of Volubilis to Valerius Concordius, a former governor and patron of the city.

EDCS-24700520.translation: To the Divine Shades. Collegium Mercuri vestiariorum (the guild of Mercury's cloth merchants) erected this at their expense to Caius Cornelius Severus, who lived 11 years.
EDCS-24700520.summary: This is a funerary monument for a child named Caius Cornelius Severus, erected by the guild of cloth merchants associated with Mercury.

EDCS-24700521.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Quintus Aelius Severus, aged 28, a deserving husband, erected by Balena.
EDCS-24700521.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Balena to her husband, Quintus Aelius Severus, who died at the age of 28.

EDCS-24700522.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. To Aemilius Iulianus, who lived 35 years.
EDCS-24700522.summary: This is a funerary monument for Aemilius Iulianus, who died at the age of 35.

EDCS-24700523.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aleia Valeriana, who lived 23 years, 11 months, and 20 days; erected by an unhappy father.
EDCS-24700523.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by a father to his deceased daughter, Aleia Valeriana, who died at the age of 23.

EDCS-24700524.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Antonius Ni lived 68 years. Iulia Mac erected this to her husband.
EDCS-24700524.summary: This is a funerary monument erected by Iulia Mac to her husband, Antonius Ni, who died at the age of 68.

EDCS-24700525.summary: This is a funerary dedication by Sextus Antonius Valerianus to someone named Antoniana.

EDCS-24700527.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Lucius Aurelius N conus lived about 40 years.
EDCS-24700527.summary: This is a funerary monument for Lucius Aurelius N conus, who died at approximately 40 years of age.

EDCS-24700528.translation: Marcus Aurelius Varus lived 31 years. Germana placed this for her husband.
EDCS-24700528.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Marcus Aurelius Varus, placed by his wife Germana.

EDCS-24700529.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Adlectus lies here; he lived 65 years.
EDCS-24700529.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Adlectus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-24700530.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Celsus lived 21 years.
EDCS-24700530.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilius Celsus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-24700531.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Caecilius Clemens, patron, made this for his well-deserving Caecilia Iusta.
EDCS-24700531.summary: This is a funerary inscription honoring Caecilia Iusta, dedicated by her patron Caecilius Clemens.

EDCS-24700532.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Caecilius Silvanus, deceased, aged 21 years.
EDCS-24700532.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Caecilius Silvanus, recording his age at death.

EDCS-24700533.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Lucius Cassius Valerianus lived 21 years and [some] months.
EDCS-24700533.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Lucius Cassius Valerianus, recording his lifespan.

EDCS-24700534.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Claudius Vitalis lived 53 years, 6 months, and 15 days.
EDCS-24700534.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Claudius Vitalis, recording his precise lifespan.

EDCS-24700535.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. gio Cogitato, 21 months old. N S Marcus Berna.
EDCS-24700535.summary: This is a funerary inscription for gio Cogitato, who died at 21 months of age, with Marcus Berna mentioned.

EDCS-24700536.translation: To Fabia Rusticula, his sister. Lucius Fabius Saturninus [erected this].
EDCS-24700536.summary: This is a funerary inscription by Lucius Fabius Saturninus for his sister Fabia Rusticula.

EDCS-24700537.translation: For Caecilia Felicissima, her son placed this. She lived 27 years.
EDCS-24700537.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Caecilia Felicissima, erected by her son and noting her lifespan.

EDCS-24700538.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Felicla, aged 25. May the earth be light upon her.
EDCS-24700538.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Felicla, who died at the age of 25.

EDCS-24700539.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Gabinia Compitaria, who died at the age of 17.

EDCS-24700540.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. For Cecilia Ianuaria, who lived 30 years. Her husband, Caelius Iabra, made this.
EDCS-24700540.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Caelius Iabra to his wife, Cecilia Ianuaria, who died at the age of 30.
