EDCS-24700541.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulius Fortunatus lived 55 years. Iulius Felix, his patron, placed this for him, who deserved well.
EDCS-24700541.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Iulius Felix to his freedman, Iulius Fortunatus, who died at the age of 55.

EDCS-24700542.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Iulia Gaia lived 30 years. Her devoted husband placed this.
EDCS-24700542.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by the devoted husband to his wife, Iulia Gaia, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-24700543.translation: For Marcia Marcellina, mother of Claudia Procula. Quintus Claudius Saturninus, Claudia Procula's heir, placed this according to her will.
EDCS-24700543.summary: This funerary epitaph records that Quintus Claudius Saturninus, heir to Claudia Procula, placed this monument for Marcia Marcellina, mother of Claudia Procula, in accordance with her will.

EDCS-24700544.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Valerius Manar lived 65 years and 5 months. Antonia Ianuaria made this for her most devoted husband.
EDCS-24700544.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Antonia Ianuaria to her devoted husband, Valerius Manar, who died at the age of 65 years and 5 months.

EDCS-24700545.translation: Maximina lived 35 years, 3 months, and 40 days. Lucius Caecilius Maurus placed this for his deserving aunt.
EDCS-24700545.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Lucius Caecilius Maurus to his deserving aunt, Maximina, who died at the age of 35 years, 3 months, and 40 days.

EDCS-24700546.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Niceni, aged 22 years and 1 month. Publius Ritius, his father, placed this.
EDCS-24700546.summary: This funerary epitaph was dedicated by Publius Ritius to his son, Niceni, who died at the age of 22 years and 1 month.

EDCS-24700548.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. Ocratia Antoniana lived 60 years.
EDCS-24700548.summary: This funerary epitaph records the life of Ocratia Antoniana, who died at the age of 60.

EDCS-24700549.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Pompeius Attalius lived for 61 years.
EDCS-24700549.summary: This epitaph records the death of Pompeius Attalius at the age of 61.

EDCS-24700550.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. For Marcus Sabinius, and for Rufina, his twin daughter.
EDCS-24700550.summary: This epitaph is dedicated to Marcus Sabinius and his twin daughter Rufina.

EDCS-24700551.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Sulpicius Victorinus lived for 13 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-24700551.summary: This epitaph records the death of Sulpicius Victorinus at the age of 13 and includes a standard wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-24700552.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Gaius V. Can, aged 10, made this tomb for himself and his family.
EDCS-24700552.summary: This epitaph records a tomb made by Gaius V. Can, who died at the age of 10, for himself and his family.

EDCS-24700553.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valerius Katellus lived for 40 years. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-24700553.summary: This epitaph records the death of Valerius Katellus at the age of 40 and includes a standard wish for a peaceful afterlife.

EDCS-24700554.translation: Lucius Valerius Caecilianus and Maria Liciniana, his parents, set this up for their most loving son, Lucius Valerius Licinianus Volubilitanus, aged 3 years, 1 month, and 20 days.
EDCS-24700554.summary: This epitaph is a memorial set up by parents for their son Lucius Valerius Licinianus Volubilitanus, who died at the age of 3 years, 1 month, and 20 days.

EDCS-24700555.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valerius Victor, son of Valerius Silvanus, lived for [uncertain age].
EDCS-24700555.summary: This epitaph records the death of Valerius Victor, son of Valerius Silvanus, with an uncertain age indicated.

EDCS-24700556.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Valeria Cogitata, aged 3 years. Her most unhappy father Cogitatus [set this up].
EDCS-24700556.summary: This epitaph is a memorial set up by a father for his daughter Valeria Cogitata, who died at the age of 3.

EDCS-24700557.translation: Sacred to the Divine Spirits. Victor lived for 6 and a half years.
EDCS-24700557.summary: This epitaph records the death of Victor at the age of 6 and a half years.

EDCS-24700558.translation: AE IIA lived for [unknown number] months, 5 [days?]. Caecilius Maurus the father and Ancana set this up. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-24700558.summary: This epitaph records a memorial set up by Caecilius Maurus and Ancana, with an incomplete age specified for AE IIA.

EDCS-24700559.translation: Titus Publius’ father and Antonia Amatia, his mother, [dedicated this] to Antonius Junior and O. T. R. [---]
EDCS-24700559.summary: This inscription appears to be a dedication to a son named Antonius Junior by his parents, Titus Publius' father and Antonia Amatia.

EDCS-24700560.translation: V. M. D. son, O. O. lus Sallustianus [dedicated this]
EDCS-24700560.summary: This inscription seems to be a dedication by a person named V. M. D. son, identified as O. O. lus Sallustianus.

EDCS-24700561.translation: To the departed spirits. Decimus Oratius lived for 20 years.
EDCS-24700561.summary: This is a funerary inscription recording the death of Decimus Oratius at the age of 20.

EDCS-24700562.translation: To the departed spirits. Emiliana lived for 15 years. Ianuaria, her mother, placed this.
EDCS-24700562.summary: This funerary inscription records that Emiliana lived for 15 years and was commemorated by her mother, Ianuaria.

EDCS-24700563.translation: Sacred to the departed spirits. [He/She] lived for 30 years. [His/Her] wife placed this.
EDCS-24700563.summary: This funerary inscription records that the deceased lived for 30 years and was commemorated by his wife.

EDCS-24700564.translation: [She] lived for 70 years. [He/She] placed this for [his/her] most dutiful mother.
EDCS-24700564.summary: This inscription is a dedication to a "most dutiful mother" who lived for 70 years, placed by an unnamed individual.

EDCS-25001055.translation: To the departed spirits. Venetus Aesuvi, a soldier of the II Cohort of Astures, from the scouts of the Britanniciani.
EDCS-25001055.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Venetus Aesuvi, a soldier who served as a scout in the II Cohort of Astures in Britannia.

EDCS-25600695.translation: Quintus Caecilius Domitianus, son of Quintus, a decurion of the municipium of Volubilis, aged 20. Quintus Caecilius Seneca Gracilis and Caecilia Antoniana, parents, placed this for their most dutiful son.
EDCS-25600695.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Quintus Caecilius Domitianus, a 20-year-old decurion of Volubilis, erected by his parents.

EDCS-25600696.translation: Lucius Fabius Crispus, father, placed this for his most dutiful son, Marcus Fabius Rogatus, son of Lucius, aged 17.
EDCS-25600696.summary: This funerary epitaph commemorates Marcus Fabius Rogatus, aged 17, erected by his father, Lucius Fabius Crispus.

EDCS-26800199.summary: This inscription likely refers to a dedication or monument associated with Emperor Augustus, possibly dating to his reign.

EDCS-26801206.translation: Marcus Salinator Quadratus, aged 85, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-26801206.summary: A funerary inscription commemorating Marcus Salinator Quadratus, dated by his age to the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-27100221.translation: To the Divine Shades. Antonius Proclinus, a cavalryman from the vexillation of the Flavian Wing, from the select guard, lived 40 years, is laid here. May the earth lie lightly upon you.
EDCS-27100221.summary: A funerary inscription for Antonius Proclinus, a cavalryman, likely from the Roman Imperial period, mentioning his military service.

EDCS-27100224.translation: Crementia, a servant of Christ, lived 37 years, 5 months, and 11 days. She rested in peace. Potentianus, grieving.
EDCS-27100224.summary: A Christian funerary inscription for Crementia, providing her lifespan and mentioning her grieving husband, Potentianus, likely from the Late Roman period.

EDCS-27100225.translation: Silius Nasonis lived 51 years. Crispus rests in this tomb.
EDCS-27100225.summary: A funerary inscription for Silius Nasonis, likely from the Roman Imperial period, stating his age and identifying Crispus as the person interred.

EDCS-27100228.translation: To the Genius of the place. Flavius Neon, prefect of the cohort of Astures and Callaeci, constructed and made the praetorium from the ground up by the hands of his fellow soldiers.
EDCS-27100228.summary: An inscription recording the construction of a praetorium (commandant's building) by Flavius Neon, prefect of a cohort, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33500239.summary: A simple funerary epitaph for Thrax Aurelius Diza, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33500255.summary: A funerary epitaph for Aelianus, stating his age, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33500268.translation: elius S atius lived 1 year and nine months. Sonnius Se es, his father, placed [this] for his devoted son.
EDCS-33500268.summary: A funerary inscription for a young child, elius S atius, set up by his father, Sonnius Se es, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33600448.summary: A funerary epitaph for Lucius Tetius Samia, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33700109.summary: An inscription identifying the workshop of a craftsman named Iucundus, likely from the Roman Imperial period.

EDCS-33700110.translation: Of Iucundus, son of Sextus Valerius.
EDCS-33700110.summary: This inscription is a dedication by Iucundus, son of Sextus Valerius, likely to a deity or for funerary purposes.

EDCS-33700487.summary: This inscription is a dedication or ownership marker by Publius Cornelius.

EDCS-33700620.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Venetus, a soldier of the II Cohort of Astures, sent back from the British expedition.
EDCS-33700620.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Venetus, a soldier from the II Cohort of Astures, who was sent back from Britain, likely dating to the Roman occupation of Britain.

EDCS-39200522.translation: For the safety and victory of the rule, the Manaphinoi dedicated this to the god Manaphos and the god Theandrios.
EDCS-39200522.summary: This is a dedication by the Manaphinoi to the gods Manaphos and Theandrios for the safety and victory of the ruling power, possibly indicating a syncretic religious practice.

EDCS-39200523.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Aurelia Sabeina Syra, who lived 55 years, for Celestina, her daughter, for eternal remembrance.
EDCS-39200523.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Aurelia Sabeina Syra, who lived 55 years, dedicated by her daughter Celestina.

EDCS-39200524.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. Magni, son of Alapitos, of Mazabana, aged 30, lies here.
EDCS-39200524.summary: This is a funerary inscription for Magni, son of Alapitos, of Mazabana, who died at the age of 30.

EDCS-46300222.translation: Of Gaius Ponticienus, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe.
EDCS-46300222.summary: This is likely a dedication or ownership marker identifying Gaius Ponticienus, son of Marcus, belonging to the Fabia tribe.

EDCS-46700006.translation: Of Gaius Pontilienus, son of Marcus, of the Fabia tribe.
EDCS-46700006.summary: This is likely a dedication or ownership marker identifying Gaius Pontilienus, son of Marcus, belonging to the Fabia tribe.

EDCS-46900032.translation: Sacred to the Divine Shades. For Caecilius Clemens, son of Titus, who deserved well. Caecilius.
EDCS-46900032.summary: This is a funerary inscription dedicated by Caecilius for his deserving relative, Caecilius Clemens, son of Titus.

EDCS-47200390.translation: From the workshop of Novitius Lu Sem.
EDCS-47200390.summary: This inscription records the origin or ownership of an object from the workshop of Novitius Lu Sem.

EDCS-47200396.summary: This is a naming inscription, likely a funerary monument, for Marcus Romulus Isce.

EDCS-47400384.summary: This inscription records a sacred dedication to the deified Victoria, with names likely of the dedicator(s).

EDCS-47400390.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Lucius Vivatius Maximus lived 33 years, 3 months, and 10 days. Iulia Maxima made this for her brother.
EDCS-47400390.summary: This is a funerary epitaph recording the lifespan of Lucius Vivatius Maximus, dedicated by his sister Iulia Maxima.

EDCS-47500001.summary: This is a partial funerary inscription for Domitia, indicating her age and a commendation.

EDCS-47500654.summary: This is a fragmentary inscription, likely a funerary dedication or property marker, for Lucius Iunius Faustus.

EDCS-55701662.translation: Sacred to Neptune Augustus. Marcus Iulius Silo, a veteran and former decurion.
EDCS-55701662.summary: This inscription records a dedication to Neptune Augustus by Marcus Iulius Silo, a veteran and former decurion.

EDCS-55701665.summary: This is a brief inscription, likely funerary, stating the name of the deceased, Gaius Annius.

EDCS-77400021.translation: Sacred to the Spirits of the Dead. Antoni Sabbatrai lived 3 years, 5 months, and 4 days. His most devoted father made this.
EDCS-77400021.summary: This is a funerary epitaph for Antoni Sabbatrai, recording their short lifespan and dedicated by their devoted father.
